title: trifolium repens subsp. ochranthum
intro: Trifolium repens subsp. ochranthum, commonly known as white clover, is a perennial legume that belongs to the pea family and is characterized by its small white flowers and trifoliate leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of white clover include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting cardiovascular health due to its high levels of isoflavones and other nutrients. White clover can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
